subject: Cloud Computing
title: Cloud and Antitrust Regulation
description: A 60-second lesson on Cloud and Antitrust Regulation.

hook.text: Three companies control 66% of the cloud market. Regulators in the U.S. and Europe are taking notice.

buildup.text: The UK's CMA and EU regulators are investigating whether cloud market concentration harms competition.

discovery.text: Key concerns: egress fees that trap customers, bundling practices, and licensing restrictions.

twist.text: Cloud providers argue the market is competitive and prices keep falling. Regulators aren't convinced.

climax.text: Antitrust action could reshape the cloud industry: banning egress fees, forcing interoperability, or both.

punchline.text: Cloud giants grew fast. Now regulators want to set the rules.

quiz.question: What specific cloud practices are regulators investigating?
options: Egress fees, bundling, and licensing restrictions that may trap customers
Cloud providers offering prices that are too low for competitors
The physical location of data centers in residential areas
